title: calophyllum incumbens
intro: Calophyllum incumbens, also known as the 'Incumbens tree', is a species of plant in the family Calophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil extracted from its seeds has been used to treat various health benefits, including wound healing, reducing inflammation, and combating fungal infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The herbal preparation of Calophyllum incumbens involves extracting the oil from its seeds, which can be used in various forms, including topical creams and ointments, and oral supplements, depending on the intended use.
